At 2030 B/16, HMS United established patrol in the vicinity of the 2030 B/16 estimated position in accordance with the Flag Officer in Command Gibraltar's signal timed 1401 B/16. HMS United patrolled in that position until 0030 B/17 in accordance with the above signal.
In a signal timed 1539 B/16, the sailing orders were amended and HMS United was now to pass through position 'T' at 0500 B/17, 'Q' at 0310 B/18, 'R' at 0520 B/19 and 'O' at 2100 B/21.

16 Sep 1943 In a signal timed 1809 A/16, the Flag Officer Submarines (F.O.S.) sent the following orders to HMS Unison (Lt. T.E. Barlow, RN) and HMS United (Lt. J.C.Y. Roxburgh, DSC, RN); 1) From position 'S' (43°01'N, 10°28'W), HMS Unison proceed dived by day via route 'Rose' to make rendezvous with the escort in position 'YY', 180° - Lizard - 10 miles at 0900 A/27 thence to Plymouth. 2) HMS United is to proceed from position 'O' (43°01'N, 10°01'W), dived by day via route 'Gold' to point 'Q' thence direct to carry out an anti-Uboat patrol in zone 'J 7'. Onward route will be signalled later. 3) Bombing restrictions will follow. (57)

17 Sep 1943 In a signal timed 1708 B/17, the Flag Officer in Command Gibraltar informed HMS Unison (Lt. T.E. Barlow, RN) and HMS United (Lt. J.C.Y. Roxburgh, DSC, RN) of the following; Enemy submarine sighted by aircraft (a Hudson of 233 Squadron based in Gibraltar) in position 37°43'N, 09°33'W at 1344 B/17 on the surface and observed to dive. Probably southbound.
[We can not place a German U-boat in this (approximate) position at this time.] (57)

25 Sep 1943 In a signal timed 1804 B/25, the Flag Officer Submarines (F.O.S.) sent the following orders to HMS United (Lt. J.C.Y. Roxburgh, DSC, RN); Leave patrol P.M./28, and proceed diving by day to join route 'Rose' in latitude 47°00'N, thence via route 'Rose' to make rendezvous in position 'YY' (180° - Lizard Head - 10 miles) at 0700 A/1. (58)

28 Sep 1943 At 0139 B/28, HMS United (Lt. J.C.Y. Roxburgh, DSC, RN) received the signal timed 2220 A/27 from the Admiralty which reported that a U-boat had been D/F'd within 75 miles from position 46°00'N, 05°00'W at 2102 Z/27.
This was near the north-west corner of patrol zone ' J 7 '. Lt. Roxburgh decided to concentrate patrol in this area.
At 0619 B/19, a dark object was sighted bearing 225° at a range of 3 miles in position 45°59'N, 04°38'W. As it was getting light, HMS United dived and closed to investigate. No HE was picked up.
At 0625 B/19, the contact was identified as a tunny fishing boat. Although this vessel was about 40 miles outside her permitted area she was not molested with.
At 1930 B/19, HMS United surfaced and left patrol in accordance with the Flag Officer Submarines (F.O.S.) signal timed 1804 A/25. (58)

29 Sep 1943 At 0201 B/29, HMS United (Lt. J.C.Y. Roxburgh, DSC, RN) received the Admiralty's signal timed 2245 A/28 which contained the following; By D/F at 2115 Z/28, U-boat estimated within 45 miles of position 46°20'N, 06°00'W.
This must have referred to the inbound U-505 which had transmitted a signal at 2312 B/28 from Grid position BF 5725 which has it centre in position 46°21'N, 06°15'W. (59)
1 Oct 1943 At 1210 B/1, HMS United (Lt. J.C.Y. Roxburgh, DSC, RN) arrived at Plymouth.
She was escorted in by HMS Olvina (A/Skr.Lt. J. Howson, RNR) which had joined at 0635 B/1 in position 180° - Lizard Point - 10 miles. (16)
4 Oct 1943 HMS United (Lt. J.C.Y. Roxburgh, DSC, RN) is taken in hand for refit at the Devonport Dockyard.
24 Feb 1944 After completing her refit at the Devonport Dockyard, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted D/G, compass adjustment and D/F calibration trials at / off Plymouth. (60)

25 Feb 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted exercises off Plymouth during which she was escorted by HMS Ambrose Pare (Ch.Skr. J.W. Morris, RNR). Also a practice attack was made on the trawler. (60)

26 Feb 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted exercises off Plymouth during which she was escorted by HMS Ambrose Pare (Ch.Skr. J.W. Morris, RNR). Also a practice attack was made on the trawler. (60)
27 Feb 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth together with HMS Tartar (Cdr. St. J.R.J. Tyrwhitt, DSC, RN) and HMCS Huron (Lt.Cdr. H.S. Rayner, DSC, RCN).
HMS United was escorted from Plymouth to the exercise area by Notre Dame de France (A/Skr.Lt. J. Coxall, RNR).
On completion of the exercises HMS United was escorted back to Plymouth by HMS Daniel Clowden (T/Skr. J.S. Cobby, RNR). (60)
28 Feb 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted exercises off Plymouth during which she was escorted by HMS Ambrose Pare (Ch.Skr. J.W. Morris, RNR). (60)

29 Feb 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth together with HMS Tartar (Cdr. St. J.R.J. Tyrwhitt, DSC, RN), HMCS Haida (Cdr. H.G. De Wolf, RCN) and HMCS Huron (Lt.Cdr. H.S. Rayner, DSC, RCN). (60)

1 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth together with with ML 159 (T/Lt. B.D. Gerrans, RNVR), ML 183 (T/Lt. A.J. Wright, RNVR) and ML 184 (T/Lt. R.W.A. Winter, RNVR) and later with HMCS Huron (Lt.Cdr. H.S. Rayner, DSC, RCN) and HMCS Athabaskan (Lt.Cdr. J.H. Stubbs, RCN). (61)

3 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth with ML 159 (T/Lt. B.D. Gerrans, RNVR), ML 183 (T/Lt. A.J. Wright, RNVR) and ML 184 (T/Lt. R.W.A. Winter, RNVR) and later with HMS Melbreak (Lt. G.E.C.G. Baines, RN) and HMCS Huron (Lt.Cdr. H.S. Rayner, DSC, RCN). (61)

4 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Plymouth with ML 159 (T/Lt. B.D. Gerrans, RNVR), ML 183 (T/Lt. A.J. Wright, RNVR) and ML 184 (T/Lt. R.W.A. Winter, RNVR), HMS Melbreak (Lt. G.E.C.G. Baines, RN), HMIS Godavari (A/Capt. J.W. Jefford, OBE, RIN), HMS Neave (T/Lt. T. Ross, RNVR) and HMS Skomer (A/Skr.Lt. D.E.S. Mair, DSO, RNR). (61)

8 Mar 1944 Around 0830 A/8, HMS Unison (T/Lt. P.C.S. Pritchard, RNR) and HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) departed Plymouth for Rothesay. They were escorted HMS Herschell (T/Lt. J.E. Freestone, RNR) and HMS Daffodil (Cdr.(Retd.) S.F. Russell, RN). (62)
10 Mar 1944 Around 1130 A/10, HMS Unison (T/Lt. P.C.S. Pritchard, RNR) and HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) arrived at Rothesay from Plymouth. (63)

13 Mar 1944 Around 1430 A/13, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) departed Rothesay for Stornoway. She made the passage together with HMS H 43 (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR). They were escorted by HMS Blade (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S.T. Wenlock, RNR).

At 0810 A/14, near Tobermory, HMS Blade and H 43 parted company and HMS United joined her new escort, HMS Persian (Lt.Cdr. A. Ecclestone, RN) for onward passage to Stornoway where they arrived at shortly before midnight during the night of 14/15 March. (61)

16 Mar 1944 Around 0200 A/16, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) departed Stronoway for Tobermory. However before arriving orders were received to proceed back to Stornoway where she arrived back around 0030 A/17. She was escorted by HMS Grenadier (A/Skr.Lt. A.G. Day, RNR). (61)
18 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S and radar exercises off Stornoway together with HMS Persian (Lt.Cdr. A. Ecclestone, RN). (61)

20 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Stornoway together with HMS Persian (Lt.Cdr. A. Ecclestone, RN). Also some practive attacks were made by the submarine on the minesweeper. (61)
22 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Stornoway together with HMS Grenadier (A/Skr.Lt. A.G. Day, RNR). (61)
23 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Stornoway together with HMS Lancer (A/Skr.Lt. J.A. Cornelius, RNR) and HMS Grenadier (A/Skr.Lt. A.G. Day, RNR).
Also a practice attack was made on HMS Grenadier. (61)
24 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Stornoway together with HMS Lancer (A/Skr.Lt. J.A. Cornelius, RNR) and HMS Grenadier (A/Skr.Lt. A.G. Day, RNR). (61)
26 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Stornoway together with HMCS Hespeler (T/Lt.Cdr. N.S.C. Dickinson, RCNVR).
HMS United also made a practice attack. (61)
27 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Stornoway together with HMCS Hespeler (T/Lt.Cdr. N.S.C. Dickinson, RCNVR) and HMS Lancer (A/Skr.Lt. J.A. Cornelius, RNR). (61)

28 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Stornoway, first together with HMCS Hespeler (T/Lt.Cdr. N.S.C. Dickinson, RCNVR) and HMS Lancer (A/Skr.Lt. J.A. Cornelius, RNR) and later with HMS Rifleman (Lt. C.L. Carroll, DSC, RNR) and HMS Southern Shore (Skr. S. Hearne, RNR). (61)

29 Mar 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Stornoway together with HMS Rifleman (Lt. C.L. Carroll, DSC, RNR), HMS Man o' War (T/Lt. P.W. Arnold, RNVR) and HMS Southern Shore (Skr. S. Hearne, RNR).
Later the same day, HMS United departed Stornoway for Tobermory. (61)
30 Mar 1944 Around 0700 B/30, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) arrived at Tobermory from Stornoway. (61)
3 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory together with HMS Whiteaker (Lt. G.P.W. Edwards, RN) and HMS Rupert (Lt. P.C.S. Black, RN).
Also practice attacks were made on these two frigates. (64)
4 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory together with HMS Rowley (Lt.Cdr. F.J.G. Jones, RD, RNR) and HMS Retalick (Lt. J.S. Brownrigg, RN). (64)
5 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory together with HMS Spragge (Lt.Cdr. G.S. Grant, RD, RNR) and HMS Stockham (T/A/Lt.Cdr. B.C. Hamilton, RNR). (64)
6 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) proceeded from Tobermory to Oban where she was to fuel. She was escorted by HMS LCT 21. (64)
8 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) proceeded from Oban to Tobermory. She was escorted by HMS ML 453 (T/Lt. L.V. Horne, RNVR). (64)

9 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first together with HMS Spragge (Lt.Cdr. G.S. Grant, RD, RNR) and HMS Duff (T/A/Lt.Cdr. F. Brock, RCNVR) and later with HMS Waldegrave (Lt. T. Hay, RN) and HMS Narborough (Lt.Cdr. W.R. Muttram, DSC, RN). (64)

10 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Waldegrave (Lt. T. Hay, RN) and HMS Narborough (Lt.Cdr. W.R. Muttram, DSC, RN) and later with HMS Duff (T/A/Lt.Cdr. F. Brock, RCNVR) and HMS Tyler (Lt. C.H. Ranking, RN). (64)

11 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, fist with HMS Havelock (Lt.Cdr. R. Hart, DSC and Bar, RN) and HMS Tyler (Lt. C.H. Ranking, RN) and later with HMS Dumbarton Castle (T/A/Lt.Cdr. P.F. Broadhead, RNR), HMS Berkeley Castle (T/Lt. F.A. Darrah, RNVR) and HMS Carisbrooke Castle (T/Lt. W.H. Forster, RNR).
HMS United also made a practice attack on HMS Havelock. (64)
15 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted several practice attacks off Tobermory during which HMS Lady Sharazad (?) acted as target. (64)

16 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Monnow (Cdr. E.G. Skinner, DSC, RD, RCNR) and later with HMS Havelock (Lt.Cdr. R. Hart, DSC and Bar, RN), La Surprise (?). (64)

19 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Lapwing (Cdr.(Retd.) E.C. Hulton, RN) and later with HMS Monnow (Cdr. E.G. Skinner, DSC, RD, RCNR) and HMS Knaresborough Castle (Lt. J.R. Freeman, RNR). (64)

20 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Knaresborough Castle (Lt. J.R. Freeman, RNR) and HMS Monnow (Cdr. E.G. Skinner, DSC, RD, RCNR) and later with HMCS Teme (T/Lt.Cdr. D.G. Jeffrey, DSO, RCNR) and HMS Lapwing (Cdr.(Retd.) E.C. Hulton, RN). (64)

21 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMCS Teme (T/Lt.Cdr. D.G. Jeffrey, DSO, RCNR) and HMS Jed (Lt.Cdr. R.S. Miller, DSC, RNR) and later with HMS Highlander (Cdr. D.A. Rayner, DSC, RNVR) and La Surprise (?).
HMS United also carried out a practice attack on HMS Highlander. (64)

22 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Highlander (Cdr. D.A. Rayner, DSC, RNVR) and La Surprise (?) and later with HMS Loch Fada (Lt.Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR) and HMS Jed (Lt.Cdr. R.S. Miller, DSC, RNR). (64)

27 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Jed (Lt.Cdr. R.S. Miller, DSC, RNR) and La Surprise (?) and later with HMCS Teme (T/Lt.Cdr. D.G. Jeffrey, DSO, RCNR) and HMS Loch Fada (Lt.Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR).
HMS United also made a practice attack on HMCS Teme. (64)

28 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Loch Fada (Lt.Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR) and HMCS Teme (T/Lt.Cdr. D.G. Jeffrey, DSO, RCNR) and later with HMS Jed (Lt.Cdr. R.S. Miller, DSC, RNR) and HMS Tintagel Castle (Lt. R. Atkinson, DSC and Bar, RNR). (64)

29 Apr 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Tobermory, first with HMS Tintagel Castle (Lt. R. Atkinson, DSC and Bar, RNR) and HMS Loch Fada (Lt.Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR) and later with HMCS Teme (T/Lt.Cdr. D.G. Jeffrey, DSO, RCNR). (64)

3 May 1944 At 0800 B/3, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) departed Tobermory for Rothesay. She made the passage together with HMS H 43 (Lt. F.R. Lawrence, RN). During the passage to Oban they were escorted by HMS Clorinde (T/Lt. M.L. Patterson, RNVR). Off Oban escort was taken over by HMS Kihna (A/Cdr.(Retd.) A.R.W. Sayle, RD, RNR). Also HMS Una (Lt. C.A.J. Nicholl, RN) joined for the passage to Rothesay. (65)
4 May 1944 At 1120 B/4, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) arrived at Rothesay from Tobermory. (65)
9 May 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted exercises in the Clyde area together with HMS Safari (Lt.Cdr.(Retd.) J.R.G. Harvey, RN). (65)
12 May 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted D/G trials on the degaussing range off Helensburgh. (65)

15 May 1944 During 15/16 May 1944, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), conducted night gunnery exercises in the Clyde area. During these exercises she was escorted by HMS Cutty Sark (A/Cdr. E.A. Stocker, DSC, RN). (65)

18 May 1944 Around 2000 B/18, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), HMS H 34 (Lt. R.L. Jay, RN) and HMS H 44 (Lt. P.N. Joyce, RN) departed Rothesay for Londonderry. They were escorted by HMS Kihna (A/Cdr.(Retd.) A.R.W. Sayle, RD, RNR). (65)
19 May 1944 Around 0720 B/19, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) arrived at Lough Foyle.

Later the same day A/S exercises were carried out, first with HMCS Port Colborne (T/Lt.Cdr. C.J. Angus, RCNR), HMCS Saint John (A/Lt.Cdr. W.R. Stacey, RCNR) and later with HMCS Meon (T/Lt.Cdr. S. Balfour, RCNVR). (65)
22 May 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with aircraft and later with HMCS Saint John (A/Lt.Cdr. W.R. Stacey, RCNR). (65)

23 May 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Lough Foyle, first with HMS Loch Fada (Lt.Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR) and later HMS Lochy (Lt.Cdr. W.J. Roberts, RNR) and HMS Cam (Lt.Cdr. J.P. Smythe, RD, RNR).
HMS United also carried out a practice attack on HMS Lochy. (65)

24 May 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Lough Foyle together with HMS Loch Fada (Lt.Cdr. B.A. Rogers, RD, RNR), HMS Lochy (Lt.Cdr. W.J. Roberts, RNR) and HMS Cam (Lt.Cdr. J.P. Smythe, RD, RNR).
HMS United also carried out a practice attack on HMS Loch Fada. (65)
25 May 1944 Around 2000 B/25, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) departed Londonderry for Rothesay. She was escorted by HMS Kihna (A/Cdr.(Retd.) A.R.W. Sayle, RD, RNR). (65)
25 May 1944 Around 0940 B/25, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) arrived at Rothesay from Londonderry. (65)

29 May 1944 Around 0930 B/29, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) and HMS Sealion (Lt. P.E. Newstead, DSC, RN) departed Rothesay for Fishguard and Heysham respectively. They were escorted by HMS Blade (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S.T. Wenlock, RNR). (65)
30 May 1944 Around 2020 B/30, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) arrived at Fishguard from Rothesay. (65)
2 Jun 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
3 Jun 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
5 Jun 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
6 Jun 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
9 Jun 1944 During the night of 9/10 June 1944, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
11 Jun 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
12 Jun 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
13 Jun 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
14 Jun 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
16 Jun 1944 During the night of 16/17 June 1944, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
18 Jun 1944 During the night of 18/19 June 1944, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
21 Jun 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
25 Jun 1944 From 25 to 29 June 1944, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. These included night exercises. (66)
30 Jun 1944 HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN) conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (66)
3 Jul 1944 From 3 to 6 July 1944, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. These included night exercises. (67)
7 Jul 1944 During the night of 7/8 July 1944, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (67)
9 Jul 1944 From 9 to 12 July 1944, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. These included night exercises. (67)
14 Jul 1944 During the night of 14/15 July 1944, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (67)
17 Jul 1944 From 17 to 21 July 1944, HMS United (Lt. N.R. Wood, RN), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. These included night exercises. (67)
23 Jul 1944 During the night of 23/24 July 1944, HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR), conducted A/S exercises off Fishguard together with aircraft. (67)
25 Jul 1944 Around 1800 B/25, HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) departed Fishguard for Rothesay. She was escorted by HMS Blade (T/A/Lt.Cdr. S.T. Wenlock, RNR). (67)
26 Jul 1944 Around 2230 B/26, HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) arrived at Rothesay from Fishguard. (67)
31 Jul 1944 HMS United (Lt. M.H. Hutley, RNR) is docked at Rothesay in AFD 20.
[As there are no logs available after July 1944 for this submarine the date of undocking is currently not known to us.]
[Details from August 1944 onwards, will be missing from the page of HMS United. It is hoped that in the future some details can be added by research being conducted at the Canadian archives.] (67)

14 Aug 1944 Around 1800 B/14, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN), HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) and HMS Vitality (Lt. K.S. Renshaw, DSC, RNR) departed Rothesay under escort of HMS Milford (Lt.Cdr. G.G. Slade, RN).

The next day, around 1250 B/15, HMS United and HMS Upright parted company to cross the Atlantic and proceed to St.Johns, Newfoundland. They were escorted by HMS Northern Sky (T/Lt. G.O.T.D. Henderson, RNVR) which had just joined.
HMS Vitality and HMS Milford continued on northwards. (68)

24 Aug 1944 Around 1200 OP/24, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN), HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) and their escort, HMS Northern Sky (T/Lt. G.O.T.D. Henderson, RNVR), arrived at St.Johns from the U.K. (69)

2 Sep 1944 Around 1000 O/2, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) and HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) departed St.Johns for Argentia, Newfoundland, Canada. They were escorted by the minesweeper HMCS Red Deer (T/Lt. D.B.D. Ross, RCNVR). (70)
3 Sep 1944 Around 1600 OP/3, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) and HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) arrived at Argentia from St. John's.
On arrival A/S exercises were carried out with their escort, HMCS Red Deer (T/Lt. D.B.D. Ross, RCNVR). (70)

10 Sep 1944 Around 0930 P/10, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) and HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) departed Argentia for Halifax. They were escorted by the Canadian minesweeper HMCS Swift Current (T/Lt. K.D. Heath, RCNVR). (70)

12 Sep 1944 Around 1400 P/12, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) and HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) and their escort, HMCS Swift Current (T/Lt. K.D. Heath, RCNVR), arrived at Halifax from Argentia. (70)

18 Sep 1944 Around 1830 P/18, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) and HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) departed Halifax for Bermuda. They were escorted by the Canadian corvette HMCS Rosthern (T/Lt. S.R.P. Annet, RCNVR).
For the daily positions of HMS Upright, HMS United and HMCS Rosthern during their passage see the map below.

22 Sep 1944 Around 1230 P/22, HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN), HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) and their escort, HMCS Rosthern (T/Lt. S.R.P. Annet, RCNVR), arrived at Bermuda from Halifax. (70)
27 Mar 1945 HMS Upright (Lt. J.A.L. Wilkinson, RN) conducted attack exercises with HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR). (71)
27 Jun 1945 HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) departed Bermuda for the Philadelphia Navy Yard. (72)
1 Jul 1945 HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) arrived at the Philadelphia Navy Yard from Bermuda.
16 Jul 1945 HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) departed the Philadelphia Navy Yard for New London, Connecticut, USA.
17 Jul 1945 HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) arrived at New London, Connecticut, USA from the Philadelphia Navy Yard.
30 Jul 1945 HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) departed New London, Connecticut, USA for Digby, Nova Scotia.
1 Aug 1945 HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) arrived at Digby, Nova Scotia from New London, Connecticut.
Later [date currently not known to us but must be at the end of the month] she departed Digby for Bermuda.
1 Sep 1945 HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) and HMS Unruffled (T/Lt. A.H. MacCoy, DSC, SANF(V)) arrived at Bermuda from Digby, Nova Scotia. (73)
9 Sep 1945 HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) and HMS Unruffled (T/Lt. A.H. MacCoy, DSC, SANF(V)) departed Bermuda for Rothesay, U.K. (72)
22 Sep 1945 At 1215 A/22, HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) arrived at Rothesay from Bermuda. (74)
22 Oct 1945 HMS United (Lt. M.D. Hutley, RNR) was placed in reserve. (72)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
